note: The phrase corpus — seed of kitebi kilemiāt·i (the phrasebook) AND the sentence-level golden corpus (AX-43). Every phrase was ratified in a tranche document. Fields: amr = dotless scholarly form; display = dotted pedagogical; en = English; register: everyday|plain|sacred; chapter = phrasebook chapter.

phrases.en: Peace! (hello)
THE Peace (formal greeting)
You good?
Yeah, I'm good.
Where you at?
My name is Jamal.
What's your name?
We gon' see each other. (goodbye)
Blessing on you! (thank you)
I'm hungry — you got food?
I'm thirsty — you got coffee?
I'm cold — you got fire?
My grandmother be cooking the greens.
Sunday dinner gon' be at my grandmother's house.

phrases.amr: Pen ḫobizon harāy — we·pen yiteč-ḫobizon pokniku·i wi al·mēr harāy.
phrases.display: Pen ḫobizon har·āy — we·pen yiteč-ḫobizon pokni·ku·i wi al·mēr har·āy.
phrases.en: This bread is good — but my grandmother's cornbread is THE best.
We done ate at the cookout.
My heart is wide today. (I'm overjoyed)
I got the blues, but I'm gon' be alright.
Don't touch my hair!
We long for our land.
Our hearts are at rest.
There is no god but God.
Bismillah — we gon' remember the Ancestors.
The Ancestors be watching.
My heart is at rest in the sanctuary.
Reason IS the torch of God.
The mercy of God is wide as the horizon.

phrases.amr: Bin ni·ʿabira al·baḥara; na·ʿiberi wi en·ibnuni.
phrases.display: Bin ni·ʿabira al·baḥar·a; na·ʿiberi wi en·ib·nun·i.
phrases.en: We BEEN crossed THE Sea; the lesson lives in our hearts.
I AM a freedman — sibling of the Freedmen.
Y'all sing! Y'all dance! The Ancestors be listening.
The people gon' choose the leaders.
The sky is blue, the land is black.
Clouds in the sky — rain's coming.
The stars be watching from the sky.
I'm walking the road, real peaceful.
I'ma ride to my grandmother's house in the car.
The Exodus done arrived; the Return gon' arrive.
I study grammar at the academy.
Y'all ask! The answers are in the stars.

phrases.amr: ʿIrefi bilā ḏikeri la wi ʿirefi.
phrases.display: ʿIrefi bilā ḏikeri la wi ʿirefi.
phrases.en: Knowledge without remembrance is no knowledge.
The dictionary IS an ocean of words.
How many sons you got?

phrases.amr: Mamānku don wanim, we·pen sanuku ʾayn wanim.
phrases.display: Mamān·ku don wanim, we·pen sanu·ku 'ayn wanim.
phrases.en: Big Mama done ate, but my sibling ain't ate.
My grandmother be nurturing the kinfolk every day.
I AM my grandfather's child.

gaps: please (politeness micro-set: please / excuse me / sorry — coin before phrasebook production)
numbers chapter (counting drills from nefer to ḥeḥ)
market chapter (buying/selling with fulus and tajerow)
